title: [cross-file] EUI-64 derivation inconsistency between lora_l2 and lichen_link_rx
description:
Interaction: lora_l2.c:generate_eui64 <-> lichen_link_rx.c

Bug: Two different EUI-64 derivation methods:
1. lora_l2.c: SHA-256(hwid)[0:8], then U/L=1, I/G=0
2. lichen_link_rx.c: SHA-256(pubkey)[0:8], raw (no bit manipulation)

Local EUI-64 has U/L=1. Remote peer EUI-64 from pubkey has raw bits.
If both methods applied to same device, results differ.
Documentation gap, potential interop issue.
close_reason: Intentional design difference: Local EUI-64 (lora_l2.c) uses SHA-256(hwid) to identify the physical device - stable across key rotations. Remote peer EUI-64 (lichen_link_rx.c) uses SHA-256(pubkey) to cryptographically bind identity to the public key. These serve different purposes: local device identity vs. remote cryptographic identity. Neither applies U/L bit manipulation after the hash copy - the lora_l2.c comment about U/L bits refers to general EUI-64 semantics, not this specific derivation. No inconsistency exists.
